questions.question_text:
A particle covers half of its total distance with speed $v_1$ and the rest half distance with speed $v_2$. Its average speed during the complete journey is:

(a) $\frac{v_1 + v_2}{2}$
(b) $\frac{v_1 v_2}{v_1 + v_2}$
(c) $\frac{2v_1 v_2}{v_1 + v_2}$
(d) $\frac{v_1^2 v_2^2}{v_1^2 + v_2^2}$
questions.solution:
Let S be the total distance travelled by the particle.

Let t₁ be the time taken by the particle to cover first half of the distance.
Then t₁ = (S/2) / v₁ = S / (2v₁)

Let t₂ be the time taken by the particle to cover remaining half of the distance.
Then t₂ = (S/2) / v₂ = S / (2v₂)

Average speed = Total distance / Total time
= S / (t₁ + t₂)
= S / [S/(2v₁) + S/(2v₂)]
= 2v₁v₂ / (v₁ + v₂)
questions.explanation: When distances are equal, average speed is the harmonic mean of the two speeds: $v_{av} = \frac{2v_1v_2}{v_1 + v_2}$

questions.question_text:
A car moves from X to Y with a uniform speed $v_u$ and returns to X with a uniform speed $v_d$. The average speed for this round trip is:

(a) $\sqrt{v_u v_d}$
(b) $\frac{v_d v_u}{v_d + v_u}$
(c) $\frac{v_u + v_d}{2}$
(d) $\frac{2v_d v_u}{v_d + v_u}$
questions.solution:
Let distance from X to Y be s.

Time taken from X to Y: t₁ = s / v_u
Time taken from Y to X: t₂ = s / v_d

Total distance = s + s = 2s
Total time = t₁ + t₂ = s/v_u + s/v_d

Average speed = Total distance / Total time
= 2s / (s/v_u + s/v_d)
= 2v_u v_d / (v_u + v_d)
questions.explanation: For equal distances, average speed is the harmonic mean: $v_{av} = \frac{2v_u v_d}{v_u + v_d}$

questions.question_text:
A car runs at a constant speed on a circular track of radius 100 m, taking 62.8 seconds for every circular lap. The average velocity and average speed for each circular lap respectively is:

(a) 10 m/s, 0
(b) 0, 0
(c) 0, 10 m/s
(d) 10 m/s, 10 m/s
questions.solution:
Distance travelled in one rotation (lap) = 2πr
= 2 × 3.14 × 100 = 628 m

Average speed = Distance / Time
= 628 / 62.8 = 10 m/s

Net displacement in one lap = 0 (starting and ending at same point)

Average velocity = Displacement / Time
= 0 / 62.8 = 0 m/s
questions.explanation: Average speed depends on total distance, while average velocity depends on net displacement. In one complete circular lap, displacement is zero.

questions.question_text:
A car moves a distance of 200 m. It covers the first half of the distance at speed 40 km/h and the second half of distance at speed $v$. The average speed is 48 km/h. The value of $v$ is:

(a) 56 km/h
(b) 60 km/h
(c) 50 km/h
(d) 48 km/h
questions.solution:
Total distance = 200 m = 0.2 km

First half distance = 100 m = 0.1 km at 40 km/h
Time for first half: t₁ = 0.1 / 40 = 0.0025 h

Second half distance = 100 m = 0.1 km at v km/h
Time for second half: t₂ = 0.1 / v

Average speed = Total distance / Total time
48 = 0.2 / (0.0025 + 0.1/v)

0.0025 + 0.1/v = 0.2 / 48
0.0025 + 0.1/v = 0.004167
0.1/v = 0.001667
v = 60 km/h
questions.explanation:
Using the formula for average speed when distances are equal: $v_{av} = \frac{2v_1v_2}{v_1 + v_2}$

$48 = \frac{2 \times 40 \times v}{40 + v}$

$48(40 + v) = 80v$
$1920 + 48v = 80v$
$1920 = 32v$
$v = 60$ km/h

questions.question_text:
A bus travelling the first one-third distance at a speed of 10 km/h, the next one-third at 20 km/h and the last one-third at 60 km/h. The average speed of the bus is:

(a) 9 km/h
(b) 16 km/h
(c) 18 km/h
(d) 48 km/h
questions.solution:
Let total distance be S.

First one-third: distance = S/3, speed = 10 km/h
Time t₁ = (S/3) / 10 = S/30

Second one-third: distance = S/3, speed = 20 km/h
Time t₂ = (S/3) / 20 = S/60

Third one-third: distance = S/3, speed = 60 km/h
Time t₃ = (S/3) / 60 = S/180

Total time = S/30 + S/60 + S/180
= (6S + 3S + S) / 180
= 10S / 180
= S/18

Average speed = Total distance / Total time
= S / (S/18)
= 18 km/h
questions.explanation: When distances are equal, average speed is the harmonic mean of the speeds: $v_{av} = \frac{3}{\frac{1}{v_1} + \frac{1}{v_2} + \frac{1}{v_3}}$
